title: Record Schemas, Field Provenance, Confidence, and Data Quality · Transfer Build
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers record schemas, field provenance, confidence, and data quality into a changed loss-aware versioned site record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of loss-aware versioned site record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Version admitted records, field meanings, missingness, constraints, and compatible migrations independently from source markup.
Attach source URL, response identity, node or attribute path, raw value digest, transform version, time, and decision to each field.
Use confidence only when tied to observable rule evidence, calibration, thresholds, and review consequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Record Schema Version
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 6010 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile versioned records, field provenance, confidence evidence, quality counts, and corrections; release evidence binds source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, lockfile, package, browser revision, container, configuration, and restored state; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Version admitted records, field meanings, missingness, constraints, and compatible migrations independently from source markup.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Correction Deletion
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-correction-deletion-6mg81e" that uses case 3673 to reconcile versioned records, field provenance, confidence evidence, quality counts, and corrections. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.
